sections.paragraph:
ἄμορφος, ον (cp. μορφή, which expresses someth. that is in principle harmonious, as Pind., I. 4, 57) misshapen, ugly (so Eur., Hdt. et al.; Aelian, NA 16, 24 p. 402, 10; Ps.-Apollod. 1, 4, 2, 1; Philo; TestSol 18:1; Just., A I, 10, 2; 59, 1 ὕλη ἄ.; Ath. R. 51, 16 ἀ. οὐσίαν) of εἴδωλα 1 Cor 12:2 v.l.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

someth. someth. = something

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Aelian Aelian , II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-Apollod Ps.-Apollod , myth., I/II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 54
